description: Clam chowder is a hearty soup made with clams and a savory base. The best-known version is New England clam chowder, which is creamy and often includes potatoes, onions, and sometimes salt pork or bacon.
core_ingredients: Cream or milk in creamy versions
preparation_steps: Cook aromatic ingredients such as onion and celery.
Add potatoes and liquid, then simmer until tender.
Add clams near the end so they stay tender.
Stir in dairy for creamy versions and adjust seasoning.
Serve hot, often with crackers or bread.

nutrition.fat: Low to moderate, higher in creamy versions

health_advice.benefits: Provides protein and minerals from clams and can be filling.
health_advice.risks: May be high in sodium; creamy versions can be rich in saturated fat.
health_advice.healthy_tip: Choose broth-heavy or lighter creamy versions and watch sodium content.

dietary_notes.note: Traditional clam chowder is seafood-based. Gluten-free versions are possible if wheat-based thickener is avoided.

allergens: Possible gluten if thickened with flour or served in a bread bowl
